summary: Backend-only testing for new JWT auth + Bulk CSV import features. 20 new pytest tests authored in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_bulk.py and 13 prior tests re-run as regression. ALL 33/33 tests PASSED against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Verified: (1) POST /api/auth/register returns access_token+user and rejects duplicate email with 400; (2) POST /api/auth/login works for new users and the pre-existing admin@shreerajco.com / Admin@123 account; invalid creds and unknown email both return 400; (3) GET /api/auth/me returns user with Bearer token, returns 401 without token and with malformed token; (4) POST /api/bulk/clients imports 2 rows from sample CSV, partial-error CSV (one row missing firm_name) creates 2 valid + reports 1 error, returns 401 without auth; (5) POST /api/bulk/documents imports docs for a client, returns 404 for invalid client_id, returns 401 without auth, correctly parses uploaded_to_accounting boolean (true/false) and storage_location; persistence verified via GET /api/documents/client/{id}. (6) Existing /api/clients, /api/documents, /api/tasks, /api/dashboard/stats and AI endpoints still respond 200 - no regressions. Note: passlib bcrypt version-read warning visible in backend logs but is benign and does not affect hashing/verification (login & register work end-to-end).

minor.issue: Per requirement the data model is shared across authenticated users, but currently ALL non-auth read AND write endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE /api/clients, /api/documents, /api/tasks, /api/dashboard/stats, /api/ai/*) are PUBLIC - no Bearer token required. Only /api/bulk/* and /api/auth/me are protected. If E1 intended auth to gate any access at all (even shared), this is a gap. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:376-541 (no Depends(get_current_user)).
Carried over from iteration_1: returns counts only for {total,pending,completed,long_pending} - no longer references stale 'required' status (verified). No action needed unless mentioned previously.

action_items: DECIDE & DOCUMENT: confirm whether non-auth public access to /api/clients, /api/documents, /api/tasks, /api/dashboard/stats, /api/ai/* is intentional. If shared-data is meant to be behind login, add Depends(get_current_user) to those routers. If truly public (firm-wide open), explicitly note this in README / test_credentials.md so future agents do not flag it.
Optional: address passlib bcrypt version-read warning by pinning bcrypt<4.1 or upgrading passlib - benign but noisy in logs.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT_SECRET_KEY is required from os.environ['JWT_SECRET_KEY'] (good - fails fast). Token expiry default 30 days is long for a CA firm app; consider shorter (e.g., 7 days) with refresh.
Auth implementation follows playbook: passlib bcrypt + python-jose HS256 + OAuth2PasswordBearer; emails normalized to lowercase on both register and login (good).
Bulk CSV endpoints accept case variations of headers (firm_name / Firm Name), skip rows with missing required fields and continue importing valid rows - matches requirement.

Bulk endpoints insert directly into Mongo without using the Pydantic Client/Document models. Works but bypasses default_factory for id/created_at (handled manually) - acceptable but duplicates logic; could refactor to use the model.
server.py still uses datetime.utcnow() (deprecation warning in Python 3.12+). Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: Tests register random emails (test_<uuid>@example.com) and create TEST_-prefixed clients/documents which are cleaned up in fixtures/teardown. The pre-existing admin@shreerajco.com login is verified; the test auto-registers admin if it does not yet exist in the DB (idempotent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env. Auth tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_bulk.py - reuse the registered_user fixture (returns token). Bearer token format: Authorization: Bearer <jwt>. Bulk CSV column headers are case-tolerant (firm_name / Firm Name, doc_name / Doc Name). Public/no-auth endpoints (clients, documents, tasks, dashboard, ai) are intentional per current shared-data design but flagged for E1 to confirm.
